role_full: Training Operations Associate II — Provider Network Operations / Provider Enablement
ats: Lever (jobs.lever.co/lyrahealth) — LinkedIn Easy Apply surfaced; native apply is Lever-hosted
live_status: LIVE — open, accepting applications (full JD + salary rendered from the live Lever posting on 2026-06-21; 200 OK via Chrome)
yoe_required: 3+ years combined relevant experience in operations, provider relations, customer service, or a similar enablement field
sponsorship_signal: No mention of sponsorship/work-auth/ITAR/US-Person/citizenship in the JD. No explicit hard-stop. Sponsorship status unconfirmed (Lyra is a private healthtech; not pre-verified in h1b cache).

hard_stop_check.yoe: PASS — JD asks 3+ years; Jamie has ~3 years; at-level, within +2yr cap
hard_stop_check.scope: PASS — genuine L&D / training-operations role: training content design/maintenance/rollout, facilitating live + async training sessions, managing agendas/invites/attendance, end-to-end training logistics, identifying training-workflow gaps + designing scalable solutions, maintaining training materials in WorkRamp (LMS). Clinical context (mental-health therapists) is the audience, NOT the function — this is training enablement, not clinical-ops or pure data entry.

priority: P1b (Learning Program / L&D / Training Operations) — Remote-US (Discovery Priority #1 band)
angle: At-level training-operations / L&D-enablement fit. Jamie's REAL training-ops + program work maps directly: InGenius (needs assessments / skill gaps -> 3 new programs; guides + workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff at -75% onboarding; facilitating training webinars for 600+ participants at 78% enrollment; managing 20+ programs + 10+ vendors with feedback analysis) covers training content design/rollout, live training facilitation + logistics, and process-gap optimization. NextGen (feedback data -> reports, 2,000+ employee experiences, internal training) + Vestas (onboarding optimization across 3 global locations, cross-functional partnership, change management) round out training logistics, cross-functional partnership, and navigating ambiguity. Google Suite fluency is genuine (canonical skills). Gemini is genuine (canonical GenAI tools). LMS (Canvas, Moodle) is genuine but Lyra's WorkRamp/Salesforce/Zendesk are NOT Jamie's — surfaced only her real LMS/Google/Gemini exposure; WorkRamp/Salesforce/Zendesk NOT added.
fabrication_guard: Jamie has NO clinical / mental-health-benefits / provider-network experience and NO Salesforce/Zendesk/WorkRamp experience — NONE fabricated. Framed via transferable training-ops/program/onboarding work only. Real tools surfaced: Google Workspace, Gemini, LMS (Canvas/Moodle). All canonical numbers; no invented metrics.
competition_flag: 100+ applicants on LinkedIn + role was reposted — crowded. This is a DEPRIORITIZE signal (lower reply odds), NOT a hard-stop. Built anyway as the best fresh L&D/training-ops fit per instruction.
notes: Template family: re-tailored from the SS&C Associate L&D Specialist package (same L&D family, already audited). Re-angled to Lyra's training-operations vocabulary (training execution & facilitation, end-to-end training logistics/lifecycle, training-workflow gap-finding, LMS, cross-functional partnership, navigating ambiguity). Header location Portland, OR (remote role). Recency order: ODN -> InGenius -> NextGen -> Vestas -> Kronos.
